name: The White House
subject: 22,000 faces of the opioid crisis
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5f370f954c4eed0755f0e3fff41e93f32e75827847ac5240290a1b74a1c9cb09b2d9d582dd94538f2e1f71ea7ebcaa1cfdaea04e62abc3c1

22,000 faces of the opioid crisis

Last week, President Donald J. Trump announced a bold policy agenda to combat the opioid crisis on three fronts: prevention and education, treatment and recovery, and law enforcement and interdiction.

Now, the White House is partnering with the Department of the Interior and the National Parks Service to bring the National Safety Council’s “Prescribed to Death” opioid memorial to the Ellipse near the White House from April 11 to April 18.

The exhibit will educate visitors on the impact of the opioid crisis with the installation of a wall of 22,000 engraved white pills—each representing the face of an American lost to a prescription opioid overdose in 2015. The exhibit is also an action center, with resources on the safe disposal of unused pills and prepaid envelopes to allow visitors to dispose of any unused medication at their homes.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5f370f954c4eed0777e11748531e6f16ace5d6e330e3680c12be61c4715da9ee69c7bd9503dde9887d0daae3ce8bdb024882028cde1bf9b4
More information about the Prescribed to Death memorial can be found here.

U.S. expels Russian intelligence officers

President Trump ordered the expulsion of dozens of Russian intelligence officers from the United States yesterday morning. He also directed the closure of the Russian consulate in Seattle, given its proximity to one of America’s submarine bases as well as Boeing facilities.

“The United States takes this action in conjunction with our NATO allies and partners around the world in response to Russia’s use of a military-grade chemical weapon on the soil of the United Kingdom, the latest in its ongoing pattern of destabilizing activities around the world,” a senior Administration official briefed reporters yesterday.

America stands in solidarity with our closest ally, the United Kingdom. While the Administration remains ready to build a better relationship with Russia, this can only happen with a change in behavior from the Russian government. Actions like this attack on British soil will carry serious consequences.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=71484d82004d289eaf9af1e08157338e8a911ae843824a23cd7aabd8345fbee424843d0e2dfa334cb5af35b3c157e7af34cdc8ab8c58727f
Read more on President Trump’s expulsion of Russian officers from the United States.

Why John Bolton is the right choice

Commentators agree that by appointing John Bolton to be the next National Security Advisor (NSA), President Trump has made an excellent choice to confront the challenges of a dangerous world and build on the accomplishments of outgoing NSA H.R. McMaster.

In their words, here are just a few of the reasons why:

-

“We may assume Pyongyang knows now that bluffing the U.S. won’t work.” – The Wall Street Journal Editorial Board
- “On the U.N. he’s been right all along. If his presence in the White House helps to scare the organization into real reform, so much the better.” – The New York Times’ Bret Stephens
- “He’s squarely in the mainstream of conservative foreign-policy thought.” – David French, The National Review
- “Enough with the John Bolton smears — he’ll be the best National Security Adviser in a generation.” – Michael Rubin of the American Enterprise Institute

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=71484d82004d289ea3bd4523d9103314c5d25b0cd6a874577a87e60e05ed8ad24ea03243ef7ee41861cc435b1f81314f033417fa2116d954
Learn more about why John Bolton’s appointment is earning widespread support.

Photo of the Day

Marine One | March 25, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will sign a proclamation for Education and Sharing Day, U.S.A. The President will later meet with the Secretary of the Treasury.

This afternoon, Vice President Mike Pence will travel to Fargo, North Dakota, to deliver keynote remarks at an America First Policies “Tax Cuts to Put America First” event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=71484d82004d289e1d2e0d115d217c2f5b8aa7c534df1472d3bafacc8f4ddd417e92c988af0cdb72f8f10ba10d6cbff08a6d9a9bfdb05a5d
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=71484d82004d289e22e2dc2badeaa15a87cce1ac98d5d0582af52faf974dd8c66d21ab5553669ebd9d55d31f95236e8d1d2431dfcab6a56e
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4d276c9d13d50510cd62f2fac980990cbdf62a5648caa67962b7670ade4b17c7f4ceaba6b4ec4b3ab44f17cdadbe249d0f6a6fa6900e5d2a2

The earlier we can start talking to voters in districts across the nation, the better our chances in November.

Together we are rebuilding this organization to make it more inclusive. We're competing -- and winning -- in states that have been overlooked for far too long. We're organizing in all 50 states, and we're getting more people involved in the democratic process.

Our hard work is paying off -- but the truth is, we are just getting started.

This Saturday at midnight is our first end-of-quarter deadline of 2018. Democrats can win big in November -- but only if we continue building this organization and investing in our party's future. Will you make your first donation of 2018 today?

name: Team McDaniel
subject: Clinton or Obama?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Friends of Chris McDaniel
body:
For most of her life, Senate-Designate Cindy Hyde-Smith was a Democrat. When she was a state Senator, Cindy Hyde-Smith represented people as a Democrat. She voted as a Democrat. And voting records show she voted in the 2008 Democratic Presidential primary.

Peter,

For most of her life, Senate-Designate Cindy Hyde-Smith was a Democrat.

When she was a state Senator, Cindy Hyde-Smith represented people as a Democrat. She voted as a Democrat. And voting records show she voted in the 2008 Democratic Presidential primary.

There were only two viable candidates left in the 2008 Democratic Presidential Primary by the time Mississippi voted on March 11: Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton.

So the big question is, Who did Cindy Hyde-Smith vote for: Obama or Clinton?

In clear contrast, Chris has been a Republican since he first heard Ronald Reagan speak when he was thirteen. But during the entire Reagan Administration, throughout the Republican Revolution in the early 1990’s and even after the spark that ignited the Tea Party Movement in February of 2009, Cindy Hyde-Smith remained a Democrat.

In fact, she only switched parties conveniently just before the Republican wave that swept across the nation in 2010.

name: Jenny Nadicksbernd
subject: Wondering what this deadline is? (You're not alone)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek, wanted to take a minute to do something a little different here.

If you keep up to date with your favorite members of Congress (like Tim Kaine!) you probably see stuff in your inbox like **" MAJOR FEC PUBLIC FILING DEADLINE [TONIGHT."](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=DfZrbS%2BuNyQUsNJw2785mq74erkYHWkb)** _A lot._

[So what _is_ an FEC deadline?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=AIGzcOLoAc70YRLek64UypQTmDwlw%2BXW) It's not a silly question, in fact I think a lot of people aren't sure what it means - when you start to type in "what is an FEC..." in Google, it pops up to the top fairly quickly. So around the end of the month when your inbox is full of these messages begging for one last donation before the clock strikes [midnight,](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=pxUGvJiyp8bu5OYVMJq5k674erkYHWkb) it's pretty common to wonder _" what is going on here?"_

The Federal Election Commission requires that candidates file a report each quarter that shows their expenditures and contributions, so that how we are getting and spending our money is transparent and available to the public. Not only is this important to uphold necessary campaign finance standards, this also means that we'll be held accountable for how much we raise and _how we raise it._

[So: March 31st is an important day - it is the final day in the first quarter of 2018. Whatever number we end up with at midnight on March 31st is the number that represents how much we have raised in 2018, and how prepared we are for November 6th.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=anEAAuiWKuqbtfF3Uc4giJQTmDwlw%2BXW)

When our numbers go public at the end of this quarter, we want everyone - our supporters and opponents alike - to see two things: We are prepared to run a highly competitive campaign, and we are prepared to do so through the support of mainly small-dollar, grassroots donors.

I hope you found this useful - and if you already knew about this, thanks for giving it a read anyway! [And before I close, you know I have to ask just one more thing: Will you donate today either for the first time, or as a continuation of your support to make sure we have 10,000 donations at the end of March?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=SrW8iCU9X1gEzESE0xZpUpQTmDwlw%2BXW)

Thanks for being the backbone of our campaign,

_Jenny_

If you keep up to date with your favorite members of Congress (like Tim Kaine!) you probably see stuff in your inbox like " MAJOR FEC PUBLIC FILING DEADLINE A lot. It's not a silly question, in fact I think a lot of people aren't sure what it means - when you start to type in "what is an FEC..." in Google, it pops up to the top fairly quickly. So around the end of the month when your inbox is full of these messages begging for one last donation before the clock strikes it's pretty common to wonder " what is going on here?" The Federal Election Commission requires that candidates file a report each quarter that shows their expenditures and contributions, so that how we are getting and spending our money is transparent and available to the public. Not only is this important to uphold necessary campaign finance standards, this also means that we'll be held accountable for how much we raise and how we raise it. body:
It’s all about delivering real results for Hoosiers. And that’s why this legislative session, we’ve been hard at work, with a laser focus on... | | | | [View this email in your browser](http://mailchi.mp/0d317c705a85/delivering-results?e=716de7d363)

| | |

|
---
|

| Friends -

It’s all about delivering real results for you and our fellow Hoosiers. And that’s why this legislative session, we worked hard, with a laser focus on improving Hoosier lives and communities.

I wanted to make sure you heard directly about our biggest accomplishments on all five pillars of my Next Level Agenda. In short, this legislative session was all about _people_ , _people_ , _people_.
[**> >LIKE GOVERNOR HOLCOMB ON FACEBOOK<<**](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=7c74773836&e=716de7d363)
**As you know, one of our top priorities is helping make sure all Hoosiers are ready for today’s 21st Century economy. And we delivered by crafting stronger workforce programs that align with the needs of real job creators. We’re designing these programs so that they’re easy and accessible for everyone – both employers _and_ employees. **

And when it comes to the jobs Hoosiers will fill, Indiana’s diversified economy is quickly seeing a rise in high-wage, tech sector jobs. That’s why I’m excited that Indiana will soon become one of only four states in the nation that exempts software as a service from state sales tax. _This will be transformative for our booming economy!_

But it doesn’t stop there. Soon all Hoosier students will also have access to computer training in school, making sure they graduate with a foundation of skills that’s needed in today’s economy.
[**> > STAY UP TO DATE BY LIKING US ON FACEBOOK<<**](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=1ef7a7cc6f&e=716de7d363)
While we focused a lot on the things that bring us forward, we also addressed the problems holding us back.

_Our great country, and Indiana especially, has been hit extremely hard by the opioid crisis. We have had too many lives cut short, and too many families ripped apart._

This session, I had the opportunity to work with the legislature to combat the drug epidemic by:
* Attacking the source of these drugs and charging those who deal illegal drugs that lead to someone’s death with the highest possible felony
* Requiring that health care professionals check the state's prescription database before prescribing a potentially addictive medication
* Placing all Hoosiers closer to addiction treatment centers

But again, we didn’t stop there. We also are committed to making Indiana home to the lowest infant mortality rate in the Midwest by 2024. That’s why this session, we passed new, standardized Levels of Care for birthing facilities – making sure babies are born in facilities that are equipped to meet their needs.

**The last thing I wanted to mention today is something you might’ve heard about already... this session we officially named the Say’s Firefly our state insect!**

But it was about more than just the state insect. It was about [Hoosier students from across the state](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=6d7a25737d&e=716de7d363) getting engaged in their government – and realizing the true impact their voices can make.

With everything we accomplished this session, I am only more excited about what we can do moving forward. I hope you’ll [follow me on Facebook](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=2f09118c3f&e=716de7d363) to stay up to date on all the hard work we’re doing for you, for our fellow Hoosiers, and for our state's future.
**[[LIKE US ON FACEBOOK]](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=2b1105d5b9&e=716de7d363)**
Sincerely,

Governor Eric Holcomb

subject: Bring it on!

| | Friends, I'm up against five opponents and "substantial GOP money" in this re-election campaign. Pundits have written about it. News sources reported it. There's a lot of work to do to win. You know what I say to that? **_LET 'S GO._** I don't back down in the face of a challenge. When I first ran for office, I was up against six primary opponents. People said it couldn't be done. _The one thing I learned ...don't believe conventional wisdom and get to work._ I hit the pavement, knocking on door after door. I walked every neighborhood in the district. I walked so much my husband Steve, worried about all the weight I lost, handed me a milkshake every morning. By Election Day, I'd knocked on 25,000 doors -- and I won. Since then, we've faced many challenges. When it comes to passing the Affordable Care Act. protecting Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security or securing funds for Planned Parenthood, my voice will not be silenced. When it comes to attacking Donald Trump and his radical agenda, I will not shy away from speaking out, no matter what attacks come my way. South Florida families rely on me to fight for them. It's my job -- and I'm proud to give it all I have. **But I can 't do it alone. I rely on you to help.** With your help, we can outwork the opposition and beat the GOP money. We cannot back down from this challenge -- not even for a second. [_**We need to reach our $30,000 FEC goal by the deadline and we're running out of time. And I need you to chip in what you can, even if it's $5 or $10, to get us there.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fea/622b7b5b/19e4cbc0/307b8c59/3007357388/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180327z?refcode=dwse180327z_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Thank you. Debbie

Friend,

With only five months until the Democratic Primary (!!) and eight months until Election Day (!!!), this Saturday is our campaign's most crucial deadline to date.

The newest poll shows that we've moved into second place -- almost a virtual tie for first -- but the cynics will be looking for any excuse they can find to tear Andrew down and say that he's not the right choice for Florida's next Governor. But we know that's not true because we're a movement. We are making history together.

This year, _Politico_ is calling Joe **" the nation's most vulnerable senator,"** and the Koch brothers are listening. They've already spent $6 million to defeat Joe, and if they keep up this pace, they'll spend _nearly $20 million_ alone by Election Day.

That doesn't even include outside groups backed by Mitch McConnell, the gun lobby, and other billionaires who have never set foot in Indiana. Pundits here in the Hoosier State are saying that $100 million might be spent on the race to defeat Joe when all is said and done. If it seemed far-fetched before, it shouldn't now.

subject: Dangerous to our democracy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:
Undemocratic. Un-American. Unacceptable.
Friends: For years, the GOP's gerrymandering - or drawing of unfair voting districts - has silenced the voices of millions of Americans, time and again allowing Republicans to steal a majority of statewide seats even while Democrats earn more votes.
**Make no mistake about it** : Gerrymandering is a fundamental threat to our democracy, our belief in equality, and our free and fair elections. If we don't take meaningful action against it before November, Democrats could be locked out of office for years to come, left unable to fight back against President Trump's agenda.
**So Friends, I 'm counting on you to join the fight before it's too late.** **[Please, add your name right now to demand that we end gerrymandering.](https://petitions.signforgood.com/endgerrymanderingnow?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=shaheen&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fcjs_gerrymandering%3Fcode%3Djtk839-ja-nd)**
Thank you.
Jeanne

name: NHGOP Press & Communications
subject: Forrester Statement on Governor Chris Sununu's Recovery Friendly Workplace Initiative Expansion
body:
** Forrester Statement on Governor Chris Sununu's Recovery Friendly Workplace Initiative Expansion**

**For Immediate Release: March 27, 2018****Contact: Patrick Hynes (202) 321-5310**
Concord - NHGOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding Governor Chris Sununu's announced expansion of his Recovery Friendly Workplace initiative:
“Governor Chris Sununu’s Recovery Friendly Workplace initiative has experienced considerable success in New Hampshire, and his announcement today that the program is expanding across the country is welcome news. Tackling the opioid crisis requires a multi-faceted approach, and Governor Chris Sununu and Republican leadership is getting the job done for New Hampshire. We are excited that other states are sharing in our success and following New Hampshire's lead on this issue.” ###
**BACKGROUND: **

Tackling the opioid crisis requires a multi-faceted approach, and Governor Chris Sununu and Republican leadership is getting the job done for New Hampshire. subject: People over party
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
We have a choice to make this November. Progressive candidates across the country
#
Friends,
We have a choice to make this November. Progressive candidates across the country are prepared to challenge incumbents that represent the corporate CEOs bankrolling their campaigns. For the first time in what seems like decades, voters will have a new set of choices when they cast their ballots; a candidate who will fight for working people or a candidate who will fight for the special interests supporting their campaign.
Now is the time to decide whether we believe dark money and Super PACs should influence elections or whether grassroots support and small-dollar contributors deserve a greater say.
[**Unlimited campaign contributions are not free speech. Together we can amplify the voices of the people when each of us chips in a few dollars.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ac79/bed43eb/5b894bbb/75fe6497/419327818/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em180327-full")
Let's tell every elected official across the country to turn out their pockets and show us who really is supporting their campaign! If their pockets are lined with money from Exxon, Big Pharma, WalMart, or from any other industry that seeks to corrupt our planet or our bodies, we deserve to know. The candidates of the future will not fight for Medicare for All and a living wage if they are beholden to the corporate interests who benefit from the disenfranchisement of working families.
**A path to victory is possible.** Now is when we must dig in and work to elect progressives in primary elections across the country who, with the strong backing of the grassroots, will fight for Medicare for All and the issues we care about. Your contribution builds our texting, calling, and canvassing programs to reach even more people and keeps us organizing locally.
[**Chip in any amount today to support our work on behalf of our revolutionary candidates.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ac79/bed43eb/5b894bbb/75fe6497/419327818/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em180327-full")
The revolution will go as far as we take it--so let's keep moving on the path to economic, racial, social, and environmental justice.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

body:
It may sound cheesy, but here's what National Joe Day is: |
---

| | Peter, did you know today is National Joe Day? Some people are celebrating Joe Day by pouring another "cup of Joe" in their favorite mug, while others will go on a quest to find the sloppiest Sloppy Joe in town. But here at Team Donnelly, we can't think of a better way to celebrate today than by celebrating our favorite Joe. He's a hard-working guy who lives near South Bend and also happens to be Indiana's voice in the U.S. Senate. Of course, we're talking about Joe Donnelly.

**[Can you help us celebrate National Joe Day by chipping in just $3.27?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c23bd/7377f838/318c548f/51fa9ae2/3804806990/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180327.2nd&amount=3.27")** | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c23bd/7377f838/318c548f/51fa9add/3804806990/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180327.2nd&amount=10")
---
It may sound cheesy, but here's what National Joe Day is: every year on March 27th, we celebrate the average man -- you know, the Average Joe. And there is no one in Washington who does more to put Indiana's Average Joes, Janes, and their families first than Joe Donnelly.
For the past six years, Joe has worked to help improve the lives of Hoosiers from the cornfields to the steel factories by protecting HIP 2.0 and Hoosiers' affordable access to health care, fighting to keep good-paying jobs right here in Indiana, and by striving to ensure our kids have access to an affordable education. To Joe, Hoosiers always come first.
**That 's why I'm asking you to join me in supporting our favorite Joe by chipping in today.**
